title: Icon Resolver
description: A pluggable icon resolution system: register custom AXIconResolver implementations via dependency injection, resolve icon keys to SVG strings or URLs through AXIconResolverService, with built-in caching and multi-resolver chaining.
imports: import { AXIconResolverService, AXIconResolver, AX_ICON_RESOLVER, AXIconResolverDefault } from '@acorex/core/icon';

hints.title: Provide a resolver
hints.content: Without at least one AX_ICON_RESOLVER provider, AXIconResolverService.resolve() has no resolvers to call and will throw. Register your resolver in app.config.ts or a feature module.

hints.title: Return null to skip
hints.content: Each resolver should return null when it cannot handle the icon key. The service tries the next resolver in the chain. Return a non-null string (SVG markup, image URL, etc.) when resolved.

hints.title: Results are cached
hints.content: Successful resolutions are stored in an internal Map. Subsequent calls to resolve() with the same key return the cached value without re-running resolvers.

hints.title: Multiple resolvers
hints.content: Register multiple resolvers with multi: true. They are tried in registration order — useful for splitting icon sets (e.g. map icons vs. UI icons).

types.title: AXIconResolver (abstract)
types.description: Base class for icon resolution plugins.

methods.content:
/**
 * Converts an icon key into its display value.
 * @param icon - Icon identifier (e.g. 'home', 'map-cloud')
 * @returns Resolved SVG/URL string, or null if this resolver cannot handle the key.
 */
abstract resolve(icon: string): Promise<string | null>

types.title: AX_ICON_RESOLVER
types.description: Injection token for registering one or more icon resolver implementations.

props.name: token
props.description: Provide a single resolver class or use multi: true to register several resolvers.

types.title: AXIconResolverDefault
types.description: Pass-through resolver that returns the icon key unchanged. Useful as a last-resort fallback.

methods.content:
/**
 * Returns the icon string as-is.
 */
resolve(icon: string): Promise<string | null>

types.title: AXIconResolverService
types.description: Root singleton that orchestrates resolver chaining and caching.

methods.content:
/**
 * Resolves an icon key to its final string representation.
 * 1. Returns cached value if available.
 * 2. Iterates registered resolvers until one returns non-null.
 * 3. Caches and returns the result.
 * @throws Error when no resolver succeeds.
 */
resolve(icon: string): Promise<string>

sections.title: Overview
sections.description: How the icon resolver system works.

content.data:
The @acorex/core/icon package decouples icon keys from their actual content (inline SVG, image URLs, sprite references, etc.).

• You register AXIconResolver implementations via the AX_ICON_RESOLVER token.
• Components and services call AXIconResolverService.resolve('my-icon-key').
• The service checks its cache, then asks each resolver in order until one returns a value.

Primary consumer: @acorex/components/map uses the resolver to turn POI iconKey values into Leaflet marker icons.

sections.title: Basic Usage
sections.description: Create an icon resolver and register it at application bootstrap.

sections.title: Resolving Icons
sections.description: Use AXIconResolverService in components and services.

content.data:
A resolver may return any string that your consumer understands:

• Inline SVG markup — common for map markers and UI icons
• Image URL — e.g. 'https://example.com/icon.png' or '/assets/icons/star.svg'
• Data URL — base64-encoded images
• Raw path — if the consumer treats unresolved paths directly

Return null when the key is unknown to your resolver so the next resolver in the chain can try.

sections.title: Dynamic / Async Resolution
sections.description: Load icons from HTTP or lazy bundles.

sections.title: Multiple Resolvers
sections.description: Chain specialized resolvers with multi: true.

content.data:
AXIconResolverService maintains a private Map<string, string>.

• First successful resolve(icon) stores the result permanently for that key.
• Later calls skip all resolvers and return the cached string immediately.
• There is no public API to clear the cache — if icon content changes at runtime, restart the app or use a different key.

Design tip: keep icon keys stable and let resolvers be pure lookups for best cache efficiency.

sections.title: Feature Registration
sections.description: Register resolvers in lazy-loaded route providers.
